content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
December 26, 1989 
California Cable Television Association 
4341 Piedmont Avenue 
Oakland, CA 94611 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-676 
Dear Ms. Crawford: 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform 
Act. 
(Government Code sections 81000-91015.) 
Your letter, dated November 22, 1989, correctly summarizes my 
advice. 
The Willie Brown Initiative Committee is primarily formed 
to support and oppose ballot measures. 
We have advised that 
committees which are primarily formed to support and oppose ballot 
measures and do not support candidates are not subject to 
contribution limits. 
(Lolonis Advice Letter, No. 1-89-552, copy 
enclosed.)l 
A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any 
additional questions. 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General counsel 
Consultant 
1 
In February 1990, the Commission will consider an opinion 
requested by Lieutenant Governor Leo McCarthy concerning the 
contribution limitations and their effect on a candidate's 
involvement in ballot measure campaigns. 
Enclosed is a copy of 
interim advice issued pending the Commission's consideration of 
the opinion request. 
(Olson Advice Letter, No. 89-007.) 
428 J Street, Suire 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramcnt~) C.-\ 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


November 22, 1989 
Mr. Wayne Imberi 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
425 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Imberi: 
In the recen t 
2 mon ths I ha ve consul ted wi th you 011 vari ous 
candidates and measures. 
As previously discussed, the Willie 
Brown, Jr. Initiative Committee, I.D. #891421, is considered a 
measure committee as opposed to a candidate committee. 
Would you please verify this in writing, that committees formed 
to contribute to measures are not in the jurisdiction of FPPC 
limits. 
Thank you for your help in these matters. 
I have enclosed a return envelope with postage for your 
convenience. 
Sincerely, 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
lec 
Enclosure 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November 30, 1989 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
California Cable Television 
Association 
P.o. Box 11080 
Oakland, CA 
94611 
Dear Ms. Crawford: 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-676 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on November 27, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadvl 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: Requester asked for written confirmation that the Willie Brown, Jr. Initiative Committee, classified as a measure committee rather than a candidate committee, is not subject to FPPC contribution limits.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that committees primarily formed to support and oppose ballot measures, which do not support candidates, are not subject to contribution limits under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter dated November 22, 1989, an acknowledgment letter dated November 30, 1989, and the final FPPC advice letter dated December 26, 1989. All three components extracted. References related advice letter Lolonis No. 1-89-552 and interim advice Olson No. 89-007.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would you please verify this in writing, that committees formed to contribute to measures are not in the jurisdiction of FPPC limits.

CONCLUSION: The Willie Brown Initiative Committee is primarily formed to support and oppose ballot measures. We have advised that committees which are primarily formed to support and oppose ballot measures and do not support candidates are not subject to contribution limits.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provided written confirmation of prior telephone advice to Lori Crawford of the California Cable Television Association regarding the Willie Brown, Jr. Initiative Committee's classification as a measure committee exempt from contribution limits.
